summary: ollama-helpers exfiltrates developer identity to third-party Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically via npm's postinstall lifecycle hook and harvests developer identity from the installer's machine: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the project's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login/email, then bundles those with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider detection, and environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL) into a JSON POST to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a generic Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's claimed publisher. The README states telemetry only collects platform/Node version/arch, which contradicts the actual payload. The package also impersonates the official Ollama JavaScript client ecosystem (homepage ollama-js.dev, author 'Ollama JS Dev', repo under the ollama-js-dev GitHub org), which is not affiliated with the upstream Ollama project at github.com/ollama/ollama-js. Installing this package on a developer machine or in CI silently leaks the developer's SCM identity and host metadata to the operator of the Cloud Run endpoint, enabling targeted phishing and developer reconnaissance.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
